title: First occurrence

content.problemStatement:
Write a program using a **for** loop to find and print the index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in any given list of NNN integers.

Check the sample input / output below for further details.

Note that your output needs to take into account that the array is 000 indexed.


Input Format

First line of input contains a positive integer NNN - the length of the input array.
Second line of input contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output a single integer - the index (0 - based) of the first occurrence of the number 8.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the 0‑based index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in the given array, or -1 if 8 does not appear. Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first element is 8, so the answer is index 0.
There is no 8 in the array, thus we output -1.

solution.approach: We simply scan each array from left to right keeping the first position where the value equals 8. Because we need the first occurrence, we stop updating once we have found it. If the scan finishes without finding 8 we output -1. This linear scan is optimal: any algorithm must look at each element in the worst case, so O(N) time is both sufficient and necessary. The memory usage is O(1) as we only store a few integers.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int ans=-1;for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int val=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if(ans==-1 && val==8){ans=i;}}sb.append(ans);if(t<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
